challenges.title: Basic @supports Rule
challenges.description: Write CSS that uses an @supports rule to test for display: grid support. Inside the @supports block, target '.container' and set display to grid, grid-template-columns to repeat(3, 1fr), and gap to 20px. Outside the @supports block, target '.container' as a fallback and set display to flex and flex-wrap to wrap. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '@supports' in css.lower(), 'Should use @supports rule'
assert 'display' in css.lower(), 'Should test display property'
assert 'grid' in css.lower(), 'Should test for grid support'
assert '.container' in css.lower(), 'Should target .container'
assert 'grid-template-columns' in css.lower(), 'Should set grid-template-columns inside @supports'
assert 'repeat(' in css.lower(), 'Should use repeat() for columns'
assert 'gap' in css.lower(), 'Should set gap'
assert 'flex' in css.lower(), 'Should use flex as fallback'
assert 'flex-wrap' in css.lower(), 'Should set flex-wrap as fallback'
hints: @supports (display: grid) { } checks if the browser supports CSS Grid.
Place the enhanced layout inside @supports and the fallback layout outside it.
The fallback flex layout ensures older browsers still get a usable layout.

challenges.title: Advanced Feature Queries with Operators
challenges.description: Write CSS with three @supports rules: (1) use @supports (display: grid) and (gap: 20px) with the 'and' operator, targeting '.modern-layout' with display grid; (2) use @supports (display: flex) or (display: grid) with the 'or' operator, targeting '.flexible' with appropriate layout; (3) use @supports not (display: grid) with the 'not' operator, targeting '.legacy' with display block and a float-based layout using float: left and width: 33.33%. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
lower = css.lower()
assert '@supports' in lower, 'Should use @supports rules'
assert lower.count('@supports') >= 3, 'Should have at least three @supports rules'
assert ' and ' in lower or ')and(' in lower.replace(' ', ''), 'Should use the and operator'
assert ' or ' in lower or ')or(' in lower.replace(' ', ''), 'Should use the or operator'
assert 'not ' in lower or 'not(' in lower, 'Should use the not operator'
assert '.modern-layout' in lower, 'Should target .modern-layout'
assert '.flexible' in lower, 'Should target .flexible'
assert '.legacy' in lower, 'Should target .legacy'
assert 'float' in lower, 'Should use float in legacy fallback'
assert '33.33%' in css, 'Should use 33.33% width for float layout'
assert 'grid' in lower, 'Should reference grid'
assert 'flex' in lower, 'Should reference flex'
hints: Use 'and' to require multiple features: @supports (display: grid) and (gap: 20px) { }
Use 'or' to match any feature: @supports (display: flex) or (display: grid) { }
Use 'not' to target browsers lacking a feature: @supports not (display: grid) { }
